findings.description: SSRF note: fetch() to arbitrary webhookUrl is by design (user-configured webhooks). No mitigation needed per requirements, but worth noting for future hardening (e.g., block private IPs).
cpu/memory metric cases return 0 — acceptable placeholder, no false positives since value=0 won't trigger 'gt' thresholds in practice.
All 3 new columns (webhookUrl, lastTriggeredAt, lastValue) are nullable — fully additive, no breaking changes.
webhookUrl validated with z.string().url().max(1000).nullable().optional() — proper Zod validation.
Good error handling: per-cluster try/catch, webhook POST try/catch, top-level job try/catch. Failures are logged and don't crash the process.
Frontend correctly handles null/undefined for lastTriggeredAt (shows 'Never'), lastValue (shows '—'), and webhookUrl (optional field).

summary: Clean, well-structured implementation. Schema changes are additive. Error handling is thorough at all levels. URL validation via Zod. Frontend gracefully handles nullable fields. Trigger indicator with 10-min recency check is a nice touch.
